concerns.text: Task T012 (Download) and T013 (Filter) are placed in Phase 3 (US1), but T007 (Base data loaders/streaming) and T008 (Synthetic generator) are in Phase 2 (Foundational). T012 and T013 explicitly consume the streaming utilities and synthetic generator logic defined in T007/T008. However, T015 (Validation) depends on T005 (Schema creation). While T005 is in Phase 2, the task T015 is listed *after* T014 (Writer). The writer (T014) produces the artifact that T015 validates. This is correct. However, T012 (Download) produces the raw data that T013 (Filter) consumes. T013 produces the filtered data that T014 (Writer) consumes. T014 produces the file that T015 validates. The order T012 -> T013 -> T014 -> T015 is correct. The issue is that T012 (Download) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) in the list, but it is the *producer* of the raw data required for T013. If T013 runs before T012 completes, it fails. The [P] tag on T012 is misleading if it implies it can run in parallel with T013. T012 must complete before T013 starts. The [P] tag should be removed from T012, or the dependency must be explicit. More critically, T013 (Filter) depends on T007 (Streaming) and T008 (Synthetic). T007/T008 are in Phase 2. This is correct. But T012 (Download) is the *first* step of US1. It must run before T013. The current list implies T012, T013, T014, T015 are sequential within US1, which is correct. The [P] tag on T012 is the error. It cannot be parallel with T013.

Task T021 (Overdensity) is marked [P] (parallel-safe). However, T021 computes local overdensity using cKDTree on the *full particle stream* (or memory-mapped stream). This operation is computationally heavy and likely requires the filtered halo data (produced by T013/T014 in US1) to be available. While T021 is in US2, it depends on the output of US1 (filtered halo data with positions). The task list places US2 after US1, which is correct. However, T021 is marked [P], implying it can run in parallel with other US2 tasks (T022, T023, T024). T022 (Shape), T023 (Spin), and T024 (Concentration) all depend on the halo data. T021 (Overdensity) also depends on the halo data. If T021, T022, T023, T024 are all marked [P], they can run in parallel *after* US1 is complete. This is acceptable. However, T021 (Overdensity) is a prerequisite for T025 (Validation of metrics) and T026 (Logging). T025 and T026 are not marked [P], which is correct. The issue is that T021 (Overdensity) is a heavy I/O operation (reading particle stream). If T022, T023, T024 also read the same particle stream (for shape, spin, concentration), they might contend for I/O. But this is a performance issue, not a dependency issue. The [P] tag is acceptable if they are independent computations on the same input. The real issue is that T021 (Overdensity) is listed as part of US2, but the spec (FR-003) and plan (Phase 1) place it in the 'Pre-processing & Metric Computation' phase, which is logically part of the data preparation for US2. The task T021 is correctly placed in US2. The [P] tag is acceptable.

Task T031 (Binning) is marked [P]. T032 (KS tests), T033 (Correction), T034 (Spearman), T035 (Bullock comparison), T036 (Visualization), T037 (Save results), T038 (Save figures). T031 (Binning) produces the binned data that T032, T033, T034, T035 consume. T032, T033, T034, T035 are independent of each other (they consume the same binned data). T036 (Visualization) depends on the results of T032, T033, T034, T035. T037 (Save results) depends on T032, T033, T034, T035. T038 (Save figures) depends on T036. The current order T031 -> T032 -> T033 -> T034 -> T035 -> T036 -> T037 -> T038 is sequential. However, T032, T033, T034, T035 can run in parallel after T031. T036, T037, T038 must wait for T032-T035. The [P] tag on T031 is correct (it can run in parallel with other US3 tasks? No, it's the first task). The [P] tag on T031 is misleading if it implies it can run in parallel with T032. T031 must complete before T032. The [P] tag should be removed from T031. T032, T033, T034, T035 can be marked [P] as they are independent. T036, T037, T038 cannot be marked [P] as they depend on previous tasks.

Tasks T009, T010, T011 (Tests for US1) are marked [P] and placed before the implementation tasks (T012-T016). The spec says 'Write these tests FIRST, ensure they FAIL before implementation'. This is a TDD approach. The order is correct: Tests first, then implementation. The [P] tag on the tests is correct as they are independent unit/integration tests. However, the implementation tasks (T012-T016) are not marked [P] relative to each other, which is correct as they are sequential (Download -> Filter -> Write -> Validate -> Log). The [P] tag on T012 is the main issue (see above).

Tasks T017-T020 (Tests for US2) are marked [P] and placed before implementation (T021-T026). This is correct for TDD. The implementation tasks T021-T026 are not marked [P] relative to each other, which is correct as they are sequential (Overdensity -> Shape -> Spin -> Concentration -> Validate -> Log). However, T021 (Overdensity) is marked [P], which is incorrect as it must complete before T022, T023, T024 can run (if they depend on the same data structure or if the data is being written/read sequentially). If T021, T022, T023, T024 are independent computations on the same input data, they can be [P]. But T021 is a heavy I/O operation. The [P] tag is acceptable if the data is read-only and shared. The main issue is the [P] tag on T021 if it implies it can run in parallel with T022, T023, T024. It can, but the data access might be a bottleneck. This is a performance concern, not a dependency violation. The [P] tag is acceptable.

Tasks T027-T030 (Tests for US3) are marked [P] and placed before implementation (T031-T038). This is correct for TDD. The implementation tasks T031-T038 are not marked [P] relative to each other, which is correct as they are sequential (Binning -> KS -> Correction -> Spearman -> Bullock -> Visualize -> Save Results -> Save Figures). However, T031 (Binning) is marked [P], which is incorrect as it must complete before T032, T033, T034, T035. T032, T033, T034, T035 can be [P] as they are independent. T036, T037, T038 cannot be [P]. The [P] tag on T031 is the issue.
